title: Wi-Fi Troubleshooting: Can't Connect to Corporate Network
text:
Q: What should I do if I can't connect to the corporate Wi-Fi network?
A: If you're having trouble connecting to the corporate Wi-Fi network, follow these steps: Windows: Step 1: Ensure Wi-Fi is enabled on your device by clicking the Wi-Fi icon in the taskbar. Step 2: Click on the network icon and select the corporate network from the list. Step 3: If prompted, enter your corporate credentials (username and password). Step 4: If you still can't connect, go to Settings > Network & Internet > Status > Network reset. Restart your computer. macOS: Step 1: Click the Wi-Fi icon in the menu bar and ensure Wi-Fi is turned on. Step 2: Select the corporate network from the list of available networks. Step 3: Enter your corporate credentials when prompted. Step 4: If the connection fails, go to System Preferences > Network, select Wi-Fi, and click 'Advanced'. Remove the corporate network from the list and try reconnecting. If issues persist, check if your device is within range of the router and ensure there are no physical obstructions. If you are still unable to connect, contact IT support for further assistance.
